body.title: Text-cortex receptive fields + emotion as gain-function — the second half of the cortical-columns thesis
body.source: ~/.unseen_university/akien/20260704.CopilotOnCognition.txt (Akien x Copilot dialogue, 2026-07-04). Companion to [[compiled-inference-thesis]] (the 2026-07-02 ClaudeWeb dialogue, already captured). Note: Copilot framed this around an LLM-in-the-loop; Akien explicitly rejected that framing ('the LLM doesn't figure into it') — the value here is the RECEPTIVE-FIELD decomposition and the EMOTION model, NOT the text-protocol/LLM-planner scaffolding.
body.summary: Second half of the cortical-columns thesis: how a mostly-TEXT substrate wears the columnar pattern, and where EMOTION lives in it. Extends [[compiled-inference-thesis]] and [[I-compiled-inference-residue]]; relates to [[I-self-improving-process]].
body.text:
SECOND HALF of the cortical-columns thesis — the first half ([[compiled-inference-thesis]], 2026-07-02) settled trees=one-question, fascia=reference-frame membrane, three-layer starvation, warden/evaluator. This is the TEXT-substrate and EMOTION half (2026-07-04 Copilot dialogue), the parts not yet in the store.

THE KEY SHIFT — you do NOT build a text-cortex by mimicking V1/V2 spatial receptive fields, and you do NOT shard the input (image OR text) into tiles fed to isolated units — that yields N isolated detectors, not a cortex (loses lateral coherence, top-down prediction, temporal continuity, feature binding). The brain's trick: every unit sees a DIFFERENT PROJECTION of the SAME local region, not a different region. Visual cortex predicts SPACE; language cortex predicts SEQUENCE. So each tree gets a different REPRESENTATION of the same text stream, not a different slice of it.

A 'TEXT RECEPTIVE FIELD' is TEMPORAL + STRUCTURAL, not spatial. Each predictive unit should see some projection drawn from: (1) a sliding TOKEN WINDOW (local context — the visual 'patch'); (2) MULTI-SCALE views of the same span (char / token / phrase / sentence / paragraph — the 'blurred/edge-filtered' patches); (3) SYNTACTIC structure (dependency arcs, constituency fragments, POS, morphology — the 'orientation/edge detectors'); (4) SEMANTIC ROLES (agent / patient / action / modifier / causal — the 'object detectors': who did what to whom); (5) DISCOURSE STATE (topic / intent / sentiment / stance / rhetorical move — 'global shape inference'); (6) a TEMPORAL PREDICTION TRACE (what was predicted last, what arrived, the error, the updated expectation — the 'motion detector'; this is where style/habit/narrative-flow is learned); plus neighbor predictions (lateral) and higher-level predictions (top-down). Each tree becomes a TEMPORAL-SEMANTIC PREDICTOR: predict next token / next structure / next relation / next discourse-move / consistency-with-past / consistency-with-topic. A tiny language microcolumn.

LAYERS of the text-cortex: L1 local predictors (char/token windows) -> L2 structural (syntactic fragments, semantic roles) -> L3 discourse (topic/intent/stance/narrative) -> L4 memory (long-range coherence, episodic recall) -> L5 META (contradiction / surprise / novelty detection). L5 is the gap-detection / voting layer from the first half.

EMOTION = the GAIN FUNCTION for the whole predictive hierarchy — NOT another input stream. It sits UNDERNEATH prediction and shapes what every unit expects / amplifies / suppresses / binds. It is the neuromodulator analog: a VECTOR OF MODULATORS every tree reads as PARAMETERS, never as content ('the chemical milieu of the text cortex'). Five modulatory parameters: (1) PRECISION/confidence (how much prediction-error matters, how fast beliefs update, rigid-vs-flexible — serotonin/GABA analog); (2) SALIENCE (what to attend to — anger amps conflict, fear amps threat, joy amps novelty — dopamine/norepinephrine analog); (3) MEMORY-CONSOLIDATION bias (what gets written to long-term graph memory — high arousal = strong encoding; valence = approach/avoid — hippocampal analog); (4) INTERPRETATION bias (fear->danger readings, anger->agency/blame, calm->literal — prefrontal analog); (5) PREDICTION STYLE (fear=short-horizon/high-precision/defensive; joy=exploratory/long-horizon/associative; sadness=low-energy/low-precision — global cortical-state analog). Represent emotion as a node/set-of-nodes (arousal, valence, precision, salience, motivation, threat, social-orientation, cognitive-load) whose values PROPAGATE as modulators each tree reads as parameters.

LOAD-BEARING BRIDGE to the first half: the EVALUATOR's -1/+1 rose-glasses<->shit-glasses dial ([[compiled-inference-thesis]], warden/evaluator split) IS this neuromodulatory GAIN FIELD — specifically the PRECISION + SALIENCE + INTERPRETATION modulators. That sharpens WHY the warden must stay moodless: the emotion field is designed to MODULATE prediction (change gain/parameters), so if it can reach DETECTION it changes what counts as true, not just how it's appraised. Emotion modulating APPRAISAL (evaluator) is correct and biological; emotion modulating DETECTION (warden) is the cache-lies corruption path. The neuromodulator model says the same thing anatomically: gain modulation belongs to the predicting units, not to the ground-truth check.

CP1 CAVEAT (as in the first half): this is a productive design SOURCE, not settled fact, and it is doubly-hedged here because it came via a general assistant framing an LLM-in-the-loop that Akien explicitly rejected. Steal the receptive-field decomposition and the emotion-as-modulation-vector; ignore the [GRAPH_CMD] text-protocol / LLM-as-planner scaffolding — that contradicts the compile-the-LLM-out thesis.
